quote_en: The villain’s nefarious plan involved stealing every left shoe in the city, forcing people to buy his overpriced replacement shoes — a scheme foiled by a vigilant cobbler.
quote_zh: 反派的邪恶计划涉及偷走城市里的所有左鞋，迫使人们购买他价格过高的替换鞋——这个计划被一位警惕的鞋匠挫败了。
ex_en: The nefarious activities of the East India Company included fomenting famines, smuggling opium into China, and bribing British members of Parliament to look the other way.
ex_zh: 东印度公司的邪恶活动包括煽动饥荒、向中国走私鸦片以及贿赂英国议会议员让他们睁一只眼闭一只眼。

def_en: happening by chance, especially in a lucky way (fortuitous encounter, fortuitous timing)
def_zh: 偶然的，幸运的（偶然相遇，幸运的时机）
quote_en: Their meeting was entirely fortuitous: they both reached for the last bagel at the same time, argued over who saw it first, and ended up sharing it and becoming friends.
quote_zh: 他们的相遇完全是偶然的：他们同时伸手去拿最后一个贝果，争论谁先看到的，最终分享了它并成为了朋友。
ex_en: The fortuitous discovery of penicillin occurred in 1928 when Alexander Fleming noticed that mold had killed bacteria in a contaminated Petri dish he had left on his messy bench.
ex_zh: 青霉素的偶然发现发生在1928年，当时亚历山大·弗莱明注意到霉菌杀死了他留在凌乱工作台上的一个受污染培养皿中的细菌。

def_en: a state of physical or mental weariness (general lassitude, overcome with lassitude)
def_zh: 疲倦，无力（全身疲倦，疲倦不堪）
quote_en: After working twelve hours straight, she felt a profound lassitude that made even lifting her coffee cup seem like a marathon effort.
quote_zh: 连续工作十二个小时后，她感到深深的疲倦，连举起咖啡杯都像是一场马拉松。
ex_en: The tropical heat induced a lassitude among the European colonists, who found themselves napping in the afternoons despite their Protestant work ethic — a habit they called “taking a siesta.”
ex_zh: 热带的高温在欧洲殖民者中引起了疲倦，尽管有着新教的工作伦理，他们发现自己下午要打盹——他们称之为“午睡”的习惯。

def_en: complete confidence in something (absolute certitude, moral certitude)
def_zh: 确信，确定无疑（绝对确定，道德上的确信）
quote_en: The scientist spoke with such certitude that no one in the audience doubted her conclusions, even though she later admitted she had made up half the data.
quote_zh: 科学家说话如此确信，以至于听众中没有人怀疑她的结论，尽管她后来承认她编造了一半的数据。
ex_en: The Spanish Inquisition operated with the certitude that heretics must be punished, torturing thousands of people based on confessions extracted under duress.
ex_zh: 西班牙宗教裁判所以异端必须受到惩罚的确信运作，根据在胁迫下获得的供词折磨了数千人。

def_en: showing shifting colors like a rainbow (iridescent feathers, iridescent sheen)
def_zh: 彩虹色的，闪色的（彩虹色的羽毛，虹彩光泽）
quote_en: The soap bubble was iridescent, shimmering with blues, greens, and pinks as it floated through the air before popping on the dog’s nose.
quote_zh: 肥皂泡是彩虹色的，在空中飘浮时闪烁着蓝色、绿色和粉色，然后在狗的鼻子上破裂。
ex_en: The wings of the morpho butterfly are not actually blue; their iridescent color comes from microscopic scales that reflect light in a way that shifts depending on the viewing angle.
ex_zh: 大闪蝶的翅膀实际上不是蓝色的；它们彩虹色的颜色来自微观鳞片，以随视角变化的方式反射光线。

def_en: eager to fight or argue (pugnacious attitude, pugnacious boxer)
def_zh: 好斗的，爱争吵的（好斗的态度，好斗的拳击手）
quote_en: The pugnacious little terrier barked at every passerby, even though it was small enough to be carried in a handbag and had once been chased away by a particularly aggressive squirrel.
quote_zh: 这只爱斗的小梗犬对着每个路人吠叫，尽管它小得可以放进手提包，而且曾经被一只特别好斗的松鼠赶跑过。
ex_en: The British politician Winston Churchill was known for his pugnacious speeches, which often insulted his opponents with memorable phrases like “a modest little person with much to be modest about.”
ex_zh: 英国政治家温斯顿·丘吉尔以其好斗的演讲而闻名，他经常用令人难忘的短语侮辱对手，如“一个谦虚的小人物，有很多可以谦虚的地方”。

def_en: excessively flattering or oily in a way that seems insincere (unctuous praise, unctuous manner)
def_zh: 谄媚的，虚情假意的（谄媚的赞美，虚情假意的态度）
quote_en: The salesman’s unctuous compliments — “Madam, you have the finest taste in flooring I have ever seen” — made the customer uncomfortable enough to buy carpet from a rival store.
quote_zh: 销售员虚情假意的恭维——“女士，您在地板方面的品味是我见过最好的”——让顾客感到不舒服，以至于从竞争对手的店里买了地毯。
ex_en: The character of Uriah Heep in Dickens’s David Copperfield is famously unctuous, constantly professing his “’umbleness” while scheming to ruin his employer.
ex_zh: 狄更斯《大卫·科波菲尔》中的角色尤赖亚·希普以其虚情假意而闻名，他不断宣称自己的“谦卑”，同时密谋毁掉他的雇主。

def_en: lazy and unwilling to work (slothful behavior, slothful employee)
def_zh: 懒惰的（懒惰的行为，懒惰的员工）
quote_en: The slothful student spent the entire weekend playing video games, then blamed his lack of studying on the game’s auto‑save feature for not reminding him to stop.
quote_zh: 懒惰的学生整个周末都在玩电子游戏，然后将他没有学习归咎于游戏的自动保存功能没有提醒他停止。
ex_en: The three‑toed sloth, whose name is the origin of “slothful,” sleeps up to twenty hours a day and moves so slowly that algae grows on its fur — yet it is perfectly adapted to its environment.
ex_zh: 三趾树懒——“slothful”一词的来源——每天睡眠长达二十小时，移动如此缓慢以至于藻类会在其毛皮上生长——然而它完美地适应了其环境。

def_en: the ability to understand and share another’s feelings (feel empathy, show empathy)
def_zh: 同理心（感受到同理心，表现出同理心）
quote_en: She felt deep empathy for her friend who had failed the driving test, because she had failed it three times herself and still had nightmares about parallel parking.
quote_zh: 她对未能通过路考的朋友深感同理心，因为她自己也失败过三次，至今仍对侧方停车做噩梦。
ex_en: The novel To Kill a Mockingbird is often taught because it generates empathy in readers, forcing them to see the world through the eyes of a child confronting racial injustice.
ex_zh: 《杀死一只知更鸟》这部小说之所以常被教学，是因为它能在读者中产生同理心，迫使他们通过一个面对种族不公的孩子的眼睛看世界。

def_en: tending to talk a great deal (loquacious neighbor, loquacious speaker)
def_zh: 健谈的，话多的（健谈的邻居，话多的演讲者）
quote_en: The loquacious taxi driver talked nonstop for the entire hour‑long ride, sharing stories about every street we passed, including the time his uncle’s cat got stuck in a drainpipe.
quote_zh: 健谈的出租车司机在整个一小时的车程中不停地说着，分享着我们经过的每条街道的故事，包括一次他叔叔的猫卡在排水管里的经历。
ex_en: The ancient Greek philosopher Socrates was so loquacious that he would stop strangers in the marketplace and ask them endless questions about justice, virtue, and love — until they begged for mercy.
ex_zh: 古希腊哲学家苏格拉底如此健谈，以至于他会在市场上拦住陌生人，问他们关于正义、美德和爱的无休止的问题——直到他们求饶。

def_en: a difficult or dangerous situation; also soft wet ground (political quagmire, financial quagmire)
def_zh: 困境，沼泽地（政治困境，财政困境）
quote_en: After heavy rains, the path to the campsite became a quagmire of mud that sucked off his boots with every step, forcing him to finish the hike in socks.
quote_zh: 大雨过后，通往营地的路变成了一片泥泞的沼泽，每一步都会吸掉他的靴子，迫使他穿着袜子完成徒步。
ex_en: The Vietnam War became a political quagmire for the United States, with no clear exit strategy and mounting casualties that eroded public support year after year.
ex_zh: 越南战争成为美国的一个政治泥潭，没有明确的退出策略，不断增加的伤亡年复一年地侵蚀着公众的支持。

def_en: overused and lacking in originality (hackneyed phrase, hackneyed plot)
def_zh: 陈腐的，老套的（陈腐的短语，老套的情节）
quote_en: The movie’s ending was so hackneyed — the hero wakes up and realizes it was all a dream — that the audience groaned loudly enough to drown out the sappy music.
quote_zh: 这部电影的结局如此老套——英雄醒来发现一切都只是一场梦——以至于观众的抱怨声盖过了煽情的音乐。
ex_en: The politician’s speech was filled with hackneyed promises about “change” and “hope” without offering any concrete policies, a tactic that had worked for decades but now felt tired.
ex_zh: 这位政客的演讲充满了关于“变革”和“希望”的陈腐承诺，却没有提出任何具体的政策，这一策略已经奏效了几十年，但现在显得疲惫。

def_en: genuine or real (bona fide expert, bona fide offer)
def_zh: 真正的，真诚的（真正的专家，真诚的提议）
quote_en: The antique dealer confirmed that the vase was a bona fide Ming dynasty artifact, not a modern replica — but he admitted it was the ugliest vase he had ever seen.
quote_zh: 古董商确认这个花瓶是真正的明朝文物，而不是现代复制品——但他承认这是他见过的最丑的花瓶。
ex_en: To compete in the Olympic Games, athletes must prove they are bona fide amateurs, a rule that has been bent so many times that professional basketball players now dominate the tournament.
ex_zh: 要参加奥运会，运动员必须证明他们是真正的业余爱好者，这一规则已经被打破了很多次，以至于现在职业篮球运动员主导了比赛。

def_en: stubbornly refusing to obey (recalcitrant child, recalcitrant employee)
def_zh: 顽抗的，不服从的（顽抗的孩子，不服从的员工）
quote_en: The recalcitrant mule refused to move no matter how much the farmer pulled on its rope, shouted at it, or offered it carrots — it just wanted to stand in the sun.
quote_zh: 这头倔强的骡子不管农民怎么拉绳子、对它大喊大叫或给它胡萝卜都不肯动——它只想站在阳光下。
ex_en: The recalcitrant student continued to use his phone in class despite repeated warnings, so the teacher finally confiscated it — and found that the student had been taking notes on it all along.
ex_zh: 尽管一再警告，这位不服从的学生继续在课堂上使用手机，所以老师最终没收了它——结果发现学生一直在用手机记笔记。

def_en: sharp or severe; also causing skin to tighten (astringent remark, astringent taste)
def_zh: 尖刻的，收敛性的（尖刻的言论，涩味）
quote_en: The critic’s astringent review called the film “a two‑hour tutorial on how to waste film” and “an insult to the chairs that had to support the audience.”
quote_zh: 评论家的尖刻评论称这部电影是“一部关于如何浪费胶片的两小时教程”和“对不得不支撑观众座椅的侮辱”。
ex_en: The unripe persimmon has an astringent taste that makes your mouth feel like it has been coated with sandpaper — a sensation that disappears once the fruit is fully ripe.
ex_zh: 未成熟的柿子有一种涩味，让你的嘴巴感觉像被砂纸覆盖了一样——一旦水果完全成熟，这种感觉就会消失。

def_en: the quality of evoking a keen sense of sadness or regret (emotional poignancy, tragic poignancy)
def_zh: 辛酸，伤感（情感上的辛酸，悲剧性的伤感）
quote_en: The final scene of the old film had such poignancy that even the cynical critic, who had mocked every previous emotional moment, quietly wiped his eyes with a popcorn napkin.
quote_zh: 这部老电影的最后一幕如此伤感，连那个嘲讽了之前每一个情感瞬间的愤世嫉俗的评论家，也悄悄用爆米花餐巾纸擦了擦眼睛。
ex_en: The poignancy of the letters from soldiers in the trenches of the Somme lies in the contrast between their hopeful words about coming home and the reader’s knowledge that half of them would be dead by morning.
ex_zh: 索姆河战壕中士兵来信的辛酸之处在于，他们关于回家的充满希望的话语与读者知道其中一半人将在天亮前死去这一事实之间的对比。

def_en: the process of coming together from different directions (convergence of ideas, technological convergence)
def_zh: 汇聚，趋同（思想的汇聚，技术趋同）
quote_en: The convergence of the two rivers created a powerful current that swept away the kayaker who had ignored the warning signs posted by the park ranger.
quote_zh: 两条河流的汇聚产生了强大的水流，冲走了无视公园管理员张贴的警告标志的皮划艇手。
ex_en: The convergence of cheap sensors, faster internet, and better artificial intelligence in the 2010s led to the explosion of smart home devices, from voice‑controlled lights to fridges that order milk.
ex_zh: 2010年代廉价传感器、更快的互联网和更好的人工智能的汇聚导致了智能家居设备的爆炸式增长，从声控灯到订购牛奶的冰箱。

def_en: not allowing yourself to have much food or alcohol (abstemious diet, abstemious habits)
def_zh: 有节制的，饮食克制的（节制的饮食，节制的生活习惯）
quote_en: The monk led an abstemious life, eating only one small meal a day and drinking nothing but water, yet he still complained that the monastery’s bread was too buttery.
quote_zh: 这位僧侣过着节制的生活，每天只吃一小顿饭，只喝水，但他仍然抱怨修道院的面包太油腻了。
ex_en: The ancient Greek philosopher Pythagoras prescribed an abstemious diet for his followers, forbidding beans, meat, and even fish, though historians debate whether the bean ban was nutritional or symbolic.
ex_zh: 古希腊哲学家毕达哥拉斯为他的追随者规定了节制的饮食，禁止豆类、肉类甚至鱼类，尽管历史学家争论豆类禁令是出于营养还是象征意义。

def_en: a powerful whirlpool; a chaotic situation (emotional maelstrom, political maelstrom)
def_zh: 大漩涡，混乱（情感大漩涡，政治混乱）
quote_en: The small fishing boat was pulled into the maelstrom, spinning wildly before being smashed against the rocks, while the fishermen clung to the mast and prayed.
quote_zh: 小渔船被卷入大漩涡，疯狂旋转，然后撞上了岩石，渔夫们紧紧抓住桅杆祈祷。
ex_en: After the assassination of Archduke Franz Ferdinand, Europe was thrown into a maelstrom of alliances and ultimatums that turned a local crisis into World War I within five weeks.
ex_zh: 在弗朗茨·斐迪南大公遇刺后，欧洲陷入了联盟和最后通牒的大混乱中，在五周内将一场局部危机变成了第一次世界大战。

def_en: impossible to deny or disprove (incontrovertible proof, incontrovertible evidence)
def_zh: 无可辩驳的，确凿的（无可辩驳的证据，确凿的证据）
quote_en: The video footage from three different angles provided incontrovertible proof that the cat, not the dog, had knocked over the lamp, ending a week‑long household cold war.
quote_zh: 来自三个不同角度的视频片段提供了无可辩驳的证据，证明打翻台灯的是猫而不是狗，结束了一场长达一周的家庭冷战。
ex_en: The fossil record offers incontrovertible evidence that birds evolved from theropod dinosaurs, including feathered specimens like Archaeopteryx that bridge the gap between reptiles and modern birds.
ex_zh: 化石记录提供了无可辩驳的证据，证明鸟类是从兽脚亚目恐龙进化而来的，包括像始祖鸟这样连接爬行动物和现代鸟类之间鸿沟的带羽毛标本。

def_en: full of energy and excitement (ebullient personality, ebullient crowd)
def_zh: 热情洋溢的，兴高采烈的（热情洋溢的个性，兴高采烈的人群）
quote_en: The ebullient puppy could not stop wagging its tail, even when it accidentally knocked over a vase, because it was simply too happy to feel guilt.
quote_zh: 这只兴高采烈的小狗不停地摇着尾巴，即使它不小心打翻了一个花瓶，因为它太高兴了，根本感觉不到内疚。
ex_en: When Nelson Mandela was released from prison in 1990, the ebullient crowd in Cape Town danced and sang for hours, a joy that spread across South Africa and around the world.
ex_zh: 当纳尔逊·曼德拉于1990年出狱时，开普敦兴高采烈的人群跳舞唱歌了几个小时，这种喜悦传遍了南非和世界各地。

def_en: a person who is fanatical about a cause (religious zealot, political zealot)
def_zh: 狂热分子（宗教狂热分子，政治狂热分子）
quote_en: The zealot refused to listen to any argument that challenged his belief that pineapple belonged on pizza, calling everyone who disagreed a culinary traitor.
quote_zh: 这位狂热分子拒绝听取任何挑战他“菠萝应该放在披萨上”这一信念的论点，称每个不同意的人是烹饪界的叛徒。
ex_en: The Sicarii, a group of Jewish zealots in the first century, assassinated Roman sympathizers with short daggers hidden under their cloaks, helping to provoke the Jewish‑Roman War.
ex_zh: 西卡里派——公元一世纪的一群犹太狂热分子——用藏在斗篷下的短匕首暗杀罗马的同情者，帮助挑起了犹太-罗马战争。

def_en: causing harm or damage (deleterious effect, deleterious to health)
def_zh: 有害的（有害影响，对健康有害）
quote_en: The deleterious effects of her habit of sleeping only four hours a night became apparent when she fell asleep standing up during a fire drill.
quote_zh: 她每晚只睡四小时的习惯的有害影响变得明显，当她在消防演习中站着睡着了时。
ex_en: The introduction of rabbits to Australia in 1859 had a deleterious effect on native ecosystems; within fifty years, millions of rabbits had stripped vast areas of vegetation, causing soil erosion and species extinction.
ex_zh: 1859年将兔子引入澳大利亚对本土生态系统产生了有害影响；在五十年内，数百万只兔子剥光了广大地区的植被，导致水土流失和物种灭绝。

def_en: mutual trust and friendship among people (sense of camaraderie, team camaraderie)
def_zh: 同志情谊，友情（同志情谊感，团队友情）
quote_en: After years of working together in a cramped, windowless office, the journalists developed a deep camaraderie that felt more like family, even though they sometimes wanted to strangle each other.
quote_zh: 在狭窄、无窗的办公室里一起工作多年后，记者们发展出了深厚的同志情谊，感觉像家人一样，尽管他们有时想把对方掐死。
ex_en: The camaraderie among soldiers in the trenches of World War I was so strong that men risked their lives to drag wounded comrades back to safety, often under continuous machine‑gun fire.
ex_zh: 第一次世界大战战壕中士兵之间的友情如此强烈，以至于他们冒着生命危险将受伤的战友拖回安全地带，常常是在持续的机枪火力下。

def_en: the final part of a story where things are explained (dramatic denouement, satisfying denouement)
def_zh: 结局，收场（戏剧性的结局，令人满意的结局）
quote_en: In the denouement of the mystery novel, the detective gathered all the suspects in the library and revealed that the butler had done it — but the butler had been dead for three chapters.
quote_zh: 在这部推理小说的结局中，侦探将所有嫌疑人聚集在图书馆，揭示了凶手是管家——但管家已经死了三个章节了。
ex_en: The denouement of the film Titanic, with elderly Rose dreaming of returning to the ship, has been praised and parodied in equal measure, but it remains one of cinema’s most recognized endings.
ex_zh: 电影《泰坦尼克号》的结局——年老的罗斯梦见回到船上——受到了同等程度的赞扬和戏仿，但它仍然是电影史上最受认可的结局之一。

def_en: extremely old or outdated (antediluvian beliefs, antediluvian technology)
def_zh: 古老的，过时的（古老的信仰，过时的技术）
quote_en: The school’s antediluvian computer lab still used machines from the 1990s that took ten minutes to boot up and made noises like a dying refrigerator.
quote_zh: 学校过时的计算机实验室仍在使用20世纪90年代的机器，启动需要十分钟，发出的声音像一台快要坏掉的冰箱。
ex_en: The antediluvian maps of the 14th century showed California as an island and included mythical creatures like sea serpents and mermaids, based on sailors’ tall tales rather than any real exploration.
ex_zh: 14世纪古老的地图将加利福尼亚显示为一个岛屿，并包含了海蛇和美人鱼等神话生物，基于水手们的夸大故事而非任何真正的探索。

def_en: a song or expression of praise and triumph (paean to freedom, joyful paean)
def_zh: 赞美歌，颂歌（对自由的赞美歌，欢乐的颂歌）
quote_en: The poet’s latest work is a paean to the beauty of his cat, celebrating its every whisker and purr in lavish verse that his friends found embarrassingly intense.
quote_zh: 诗人最新的作品是一首对他猫的美丽的赞美歌，用华丽的诗句歌颂它的每一根胡须和每一次呼噜声，他的朋友们觉得强烈得令人尴尬。
ex_en: Beethoven’s Ninth Symphony ends with a paean to joy, setting Friedrich Schiller’s poem “Ode to Joy” to music that has become the anthem of the European Union.
ex_zh: 贝多芬的第九交响曲以一首对欢乐的赞美歌结束，将弗里德里希·席勒的诗歌《欢乐颂》谱成音乐，这首歌已成为欧盟的盟歌。

def_en: a hairstyle (elaborate coiffure, elegant coiffure)
def_zh: 发型（精致的发型，优雅的发型）
quote_en: The actress spent three hours in the stylist’s chair achieving the elaborate coiffure required for the period film, only to have it ruined by a sudden rainstorm on the first day of shooting.
quote_zh: 这位女演员在造型师的椅子上花了三个小时，打造出这部古装片所需的精致发型，结果在拍摄第一天就被一场突如其来的暴雨毁了。
ex_en: The elaborate coiffures of 18th‑century French aristocracy, which could be three feet high and decorated with model ships, fruit, or even birdcages, required servants to help the wearer sleep upright.
ex_zh: 18世纪法国贵族精致的发型可以高达三英尺，装饰着模型船、水果甚至鸟笼，需要仆人帮助佩戴者直立睡觉。

def_en: not important or significant (inconsequential detail, inconsequential remark)
def_zh: 不重要的，微不足道的（无关紧要的细节，无足轻重的评论）
quote_en: The manager ignored the inconsequential errors in the report — a missing comma here, a typo there — focusing only on the major flaw that the entire conclusion was based on a miscalculation.
quote_zh: 经理忽略了报告中无关紧要的错误——这里少一个逗号，那里一个错别字——只关注那个重大的缺陷：整个结论都是基于一个计算错误。
ex_en: They argued for an hour about an inconsequential matter — which brand of bottled water to buy — until the store closed, forcing them to drink from the tap like ordinary people.
ex_zh: 他们为一个微不足道的问题——买哪个品牌的瓶装水——争论了一个小时，直到商店关门，迫使他们像普通人一样喝自来水。

def_en: the customers of a business (regular clientele, wealthy clientele)
def_zh: 客户，顾客（常客，富有的顾客）
quote_en: The exclusive restaurant caters to a wealthy clientele who are willing to pay five hundred dollars for a single meal, but the chef admits he can’t taste the difference between expensive and cheap wine.
quote_zh: 这家高档餐厅迎合富有的客户，他们愿意为一顿饭支付五百美元，但厨师承认他尝不出贵价酒和廉价酒的区别。
ex_en: The clientele of the British pub changed dramatically after the new owners took over, replacing older locals who drank ale and played darts with young professionals who ordered craft beer and worked on laptops.
ex_zh: 在新老板接手后，这家英国酒吧的客户群发生了巨大变化，喝啤酒、玩飞镖的老本地人被点精酿啤酒、用笔记本电脑工作的年轻专业人士所取代。

def_en: making a show of being morally superior (sanctimonious tone, sanctimonious preacher)
def_zh: 假装虔诚的，道貌岸然的（道貌岸然的语气，假装虔诚的传教士）
quote_en: His sanctimonious lecture about the evils of lying was hard to take from a man who had just told his wife that her haircut looked “interesting” when he really meant “hideous.”
quote_zh: 一个刚刚告诉妻子她的发型“很有趣”而实际意思是“丑陋”的男人，发表关于说谎之恶的道貌岸然的演讲，实在令人难以接受。
ex_en: The sanctimonious character of Tartuffe in Molière’s play of the same name pretends to be a pious religious man while trying to seduce his benefactor’s wife and steal his house.
ex_zh: 莫里哀同名戏剧中的道貌岸然的角色塔尔图夫假装是一个虔诚的宗教人士，同时试图勾引他恩人的妻子并偷走他的房子。

def_en: an unpleasant or unhealthy vapor; a harmful atmosphere (miasma of corruption, poisonous miasma)
def_zh: 瘴气，有害气氛（腐败的瘴气，有毒的瘴气）
quote_en: The swamp near the village emitted a foul miasma that made the residents cough and feel dizzy, until they discovered the smell was actually coming from their own garbage dump.
quote_zh: 村庄附近的沼泽散发出恶臭的瘴气，让居民们咳嗽并感到头晕，直到他们发现气味实际上来自他们自己的垃圾场。
ex_en: Before germ theory, doctors believed that diseases like cholera were caused by miasma — bad air from swamps or sewers — which led them to recommend carrying flowers to mask the smell rather than washing hands.
ex_zh: 在细菌理论出现之前，医生们认为像霍乱这样的疾病是由瘴气——来自沼泽或下水道的坏空气——引起的，这导致他们建议携带鲜花来掩盖气味而不是洗手。

def_en: making a fault or crime less serious (extenuating circumstances, extenuating factors)
def_zh: 情有可原的，减轻罪责的（情有可原的情况，减轻罪责的因素）
quote_en: The court considered the defendant’s difficult childhood — he had been raised by wolves, or so he claimed — as an extenuating circumstance and gave him a lighter sentence of only ten years.
quote_zh: 法院将被告艰难的童年——据他说是被狼养大的——视为情有可原的情况，并给予他较轻的判决，只有十年徒刑。
ex_en: There were no extenuating factors for the captain’s decision to sail into the hurricane; he simply ignored every weather warning, costing the lives of forty crew members.
ex_zh: 船长决定驶入飓风没有任何情有可原的因素；他完全无视了每一个天气警告，导致四十名船员丧生。

def_en: spoken or done without preparation (extemporaneous speech, extemporaneous remarks)
def_zh: 即兴的，无准备的（即兴演讲，即兴评论）
quote_en: The professor gave an extemporaneous lecture on quantum mechanics after his slides failed to load, drawing diagrams from memory and accidentally inventing a new equation that physicists are still debating.
quote_zh: 在幻灯片无法加载后，教授作了一次关于量子力学的即兴讲座，凭记忆画出图表，并意外地发明了一个物理学家仍在争论的新方程。
ex_en: The best man’s extemporaneous toast at the wedding — which he had definitely not prepared, judging by the way he kept looking at his phone — was surprisingly moving and only mildly embarrassing.
ex_zh: 伴郎在婚礼上的即兴祝酒词——从他不断看手机的方式判断，他绝对没有准备——出人意料地动人，只有一点轻微的尴尬。

def_en: to criticize harshly (lambaste a policy, lambaste an opponent)
def_zh: 严厉批评，痛斥（严厉批评一项政策，痛斥对手）
quote_en: The editorial lambasted the mayor for failing to fix the potholes, calling his inaction “a betrayal of every citizen who owns a car with functioning suspension.”
quote_zh: 这篇社论严厉批评市长未能修复路面坑洼，称他的不作为是“对每一位拥有悬挂系统正常工作的汽车的公民的背叛”。
ex_en: After the team lost its tenth straight game, the sports radio hosts lambasted the coach for forty‑five minutes, then spent another thirty criticizing the callers who defended him.
ex_zh: 在球队遭遇十连败后，体育电台主持人对教练进行了四十五分钟的痛斥，然后又花了三十分钟批评那些为他辩护的来电听众。

def_en: preferring to live alone and avoid others (reclusive author, reclusive lifestyle)
def_zh: 隐居的，避世的（隐居的作家，隐居的生活方式）
quote_en: The reclusive billionaire rarely left his mansion, conducting all business by phone and ordering groceries online, but he still managed to annoy his neighbors with his robot lawnmower.
quote_zh: 这位隐居的亿万富翁很少离开他的豪宅，通过电话处理所有业务，在线订购食品杂货，但他仍然用他的自动割草机惹恼了邻居。
ex_en: The reclusive author J.D. Salinger, who wrote The Catcher in the Rye, stopped publishing in 1965 and spent the last fifty years of his life behind a locked gate in New Hampshire.
ex_zh: 隐居作家J.D.塞林格——著有《麦田里的守望者》——于1965年停止出版，在他生命的最后五十年里躲在新罕布什尔州一扇上锁的门后。

def_en: cunning and willing to deceive for power (Machiavellian scheme, Machiavellian politician)
def_zh: 马基雅维利式的，狡诈的（马基雅维利式的阴谋，狡诈的政客）
quote_en: The CEO’s Machiavellian strategy involved secretly undermining his rivals while pretending to support them, then accepting their grateful resignation letters with a sad shake of his head.
quote_zh: 这位CEO的马基雅维利式策略包括在假装支持竞争对手的同时暗中削弱他们，然后一边难过地摇头一边接受他们感激的辞职信。
ex_en: The Renaissance diplomat Niccolò Machiavelli, whose name became synonymous with cunning, actually wrote The Prince as a warning to republics about how tyrants seize power — but tyrants read it as an instruction manual.
ex_zh: 文艺复兴时期的外交官尼科洛·马基雅维利——他的名字已成为狡诈的代名词——实际上写《君主论》是为了向共和国警告暴君如何夺取权力——但暴君把它当作操作手册来读。

def_en: filled with bitter criticism or malice (vitriolic attack, vitriolic language)
def_zh: 尖刻的，辛辣的（尖刻的攻击，辛辣的语言）
quote_en: The film critic’s vitriolic review called the director “a fraud who has never made a decent movie and probably cannot operate a toaster without instructions.”
quote_zh: 影评人尖刻的评论称导演是“一个从未拍过一部像样电影、没有说明书可能连烤面包机都不会操作的骗子”。
ex_en: The debates between Alexander Hamilton and Thomas Jefferson were so vitriolic that Jefferson later described Hamilton as “a man whose history was a tissue of machinations,” and Hamilton called Jefferson “an atheist in religion and a fanatic in politics.”
ex_zh: 亚历山大·汉密尔顿和托马斯·杰斐逊之间的辩论如此尖刻，以至于杰斐逊后来将汉密尔顿描述为“一个其历史是一连串阴谋的人”，而汉密尔顿称杰斐逊为“宗教上的无神论者和政治上的狂热分子”。

def_en: a skilled storyteller (gifted raconteur, witty raconteur)
def_zh: 健谈者，善于讲故事的人（有天赋的故事讲述者，机智的健谈者）
quote_en: The old sailor was a gifted raconteur who could make even a trip to the grocery store sound like an epic adventure involving sea monsters and buried treasure — none of which was true.
quote_zh: 这位老水手是一个有天赋的故事讲述者，连去杂货店的一次行程都能讲得像一场涉及海怪和埋藏宝藏的史诗冒险——没有一句是真的。
ex_en: The writer Oscar Wilde was famous as a raconteur; his dinner party stories were so brilliant that guests often forgot to eat, and many of his wittiest lines survive only because someone wrote them down on a napkin.
ex_zh: 作家奥斯卡·王尔德作为健谈者而闻名；他的晚宴故事如此精彩，以至于客人们常常忘记吃饭，他最机智的许多台词之所以流传下来，只是因为有人把它们写在了餐巾纸上。

def_en: belonging to a period other than the one being portrayed (anachronistic detail, anachronistic technology)
def_zh: 时代错误的，过时的（时代错误的细节，过时的技术）
quote_en: In the film set in ancient Rome, a character wearing a wristwatch was an anachronistic mistake that history buffs noticed immediately and tweeted about before the credits rolled.
quote_zh: 在这部以古罗马为背景的电影中，一个角色戴着手表是一个时代错误，历史爱好者立即注意到了，并在片尾字幕滚动之前就发推文讨论了。
ex_en: The company’s insistence on using fax machines feels anachronistic in an era of cloud storage and instant messaging, but the elderly CEO refused to learn what “the cloud” even meant.
ex_zh: 这家公司坚持使用传真机，在云存储和即时通讯的时代感觉过时了，但年迈的CEO拒绝学习“云”到底是什么意思。

def_en: casual lack of concern (carefree insouciance, youthful insouciance)
def_zh: 漠不关心，无忧无虑（无忧无虑，年轻的漠不关心）
quote_en: The student’s insouciance about the upcoming exam — he didn’t study at all and spent the night playing video games — turned to panic when he saw that the first question asked for his name.
quote_zh: 学生对即将到来的考试的漠不关心——他根本没学习，整晚都在玩电子游戏——在看到第一个问题是问他叫什么名字时变成了恐慌。
ex_en: The aristocrat’s insouciance about the starving peasants, which he expressed by saying “Let them eat cake” (though historians doubt Marie Antoinette actually said it), became a symbol of the French Revolution’s rage against the ruling class.
ex_zh: 这位贵族对饥饿农民的漠不关心——他通过说“让他们吃蛋糕”来表达（尽管历史学家怀疑玛丽·安托瓦内特是否真的说过）——成为了法国大革命对统治阶级愤怒的象征。

def_en: refusal to change one’s views or agree (political intransigence, stubborn intransigence)
def_zh: 不妥协，不让步（政治上的不妥协，顽固的不让步）
quote_en: The union’s intransigence during negotiations — they refused to accept any wage less than a fifty percent increase — led to a strike that lasted so long that both sides forgot what they were fighting about.
quote_zh: 工会在谈判中的不妥协——他们拒绝接受任何低于百分之五十的加薪——导致了一场持续如此之久的罢工，以至于双方都忘了他们在争什么。
ex_en: The intransigence of the French monarchy in granting any political reforms led directly to the convening of the Estates‑General in 1789, which in turn triggered the French Revolution.
ex_zh: 法国君主制在授予任何政治改革方面的不妥协直接导致了1789年三级会议的召开，这反过来引发了法国大革命。

def_en: a huge, powerful force that crushes everything in its path (military juggernaut, corporate juggernaut)
def_zh: 不可抗拒的强大力量（军事巨兽，企业巨兽）
quote_en: The invading army was a juggernaut that swept through the small country in less than two weeks, pausing only to steal every chicken and cow in its path.
quote_zh: 入侵的军队是一股不可抗拒的力量，在不到两周内席卷了这个小国，只在途中停下来偷走每一只鸡和牛。
ex_en: The tech juggernaut Amazon expanded from selling books to dominating cloud computing, streaming video, and even grocery delivery, becoming so large that antitrust regulators in multiple countries took notice.
ex_zh: 这家科技巨兽亚马逊从卖书扩展到主导云计算、流媒体视频甚至食品杂货配送，变得如此庞大，以至于多个国家的反垄断监管机构都注意到了。

def_en: a state of disuse (fall into desuetude, long desuetude)
def_zh: 废止，废弃（被废弃，长期废弃）
quote_en: The old railway line fell into desuetude after the highway was built, and now weeds grow between the tracks where trains once carried hundreds of passengers each day.
quote_zh: 在高速公路建成后，这条老铁路线被废弃了，如今铁轨间长满了杂草，而这里曾经每天有火车运送数百名乘客。
ex_en: Many once‑common English words, such as “betwixt,” “perchance,” and “yclept,” have fallen into desuetude and are now only encountered in Shakespeare or in the speech of eccentric professors.
ex_zh: 许多曾经常见的英语单词，如“betwixt”、“perchance”和“yclept”，已经废弃，如今只在莎士比亚的作品或古怪教授的讲话中才能遇到。

def_en: luck that leads to fortunate discoveries (happy serendipity, pure serendipity)
def_zh: 机缘巧合，意外发现（愉快的机缘巧合，纯粹的机缘巧合）
quote_en: By pure serendipity, she found the rare book she had been seeking for years — about the history of accidental discoveries — in a small charity shop whose owner had found it in a dumpster.
quote_zh: 纯粹是机缘巧合，她在一家小慈善商店找到了她多年来一直在寻找的珍本书——关于意外发现的历史——而店主是在垃圾箱里找到它的。
ex_en: The discovery of the microwave oven was pure serendipity: Percy Spencer, an engineer at Raytheon, noticed that a chocolate bar in his pocket had melted while he was working on radar equipment.
ex_zh: 微波炉的发现纯粹是机缘巧合：雷神公司的工程师珀西·斯宾塞注意到，他在研究雷达设备时，口袋里的巧克力棒融化了。

def_en: aggressive or defiant behavior (truculence of the crowd, youthful truculence)
def_zh: 好斗，粗暴（人群的好斗，年轻的好斗）
quote_en: The boxer’s truculence in the ring intimidated his opponents before the first punch was even thrown, but his gentle demeanor outside the ring surprised everyone who met him.
quote_zh: 拳击手在拳击场上的好斗在他的第一拳还没打出之前就吓倒了对手，但他在拳击场外的温和举止让每个遇见他的人都感到惊讶。
ex_en: The truculence of the Scottish Highlanders toward English rule was legendary; they fought so fiercely at the Battle of Culloden that the victorious English executed every wounded man they found.
ex_zh: 苏格兰高地人对英国统治的好斗是出了名的；他们在卡洛登战役中战斗得如此激烈，以至于获胜的英国人处决了他们找到的每一个伤员。

def_en: having excessive interest in oneself (narcissistic personality, narcissistic behavior)
def_zh: 自恋的（自恋型人格，自恋行为）
quote_en: The narcissistic actor spent hours admiring his own reflection in the window of the limousine, completely ignoring the fans who had waited in the rain for his autograph.
quote_zh: 这位自恋的演员花了几个小时欣赏自己在豪华轿车车窗上的倒影，完全忽视了那些在雨中等待他签名的粉丝。
ex_en: The myth of Narcissus, who fell in love with his own reflection and died staring at it, gave rise to the term “narcissistic” and serves as an ancient warning against excessive self‑regard.
ex_zh: 那喀索斯的神话——他爱上了自己的倒影并盯着它死去——产生了“自恋”这个词，并作为一个古老的警告反对过度的自我关注。

def_en: a short, witty saying (famous apothegm, memorable apothegm)
def_zh: 格言，箴言（著名的格言，令人难忘的箴言）
quote_en: Benjamin Franklin’s apothegm “A penny saved is a penny earned” has guided generations of frugal Americans, though in today’s inflation, a penny saved is barely worth picking up off the sidewalk.
quote_zh: 本杰明·富兰克林的格言“省下一分钱就是赚到一分钱”指导了几代节俭的美国人，尽管在今天的通货膨胀下，省下一分钱几乎不值得从人行道上捡起来。
ex_en: The apothegm “Know thyself” was inscribed above the entrance to the Temple of Apollo at Delphi, reminding ancient Greeks that self‑awareness was the foundation of wisdom.
ex_zh: “认识你自己”这句格言刻在德尔斐阿波罗神庙的入口上方，提醒古希腊人自我意识是智慧的基础。

def_en: not allowing liquids to pass through (impermeable layer, impermeable fabric)
def_zh: 不可渗透的，防水的（不透水层，防水面料）
quote_en: The raincoat was made of an impermeable material that kept him dry even during a downpour, but it also made him sweat so much that he was just as wet from the inside.
quote_zh: 这件雨衣由防水材料制成，即使在倾盆大雨中也让他保持干爽，但它也让他大量出汗，以至于他从里面也一样湿透了。
ex_en: The clay layer beneath the soil is impermeable, which is why the rainwater collects on the surface and forms a pond every spring, providing a breeding ground for mosquitoes.
ex_zh: 土壤下的粘土层是不可渗透的，这就是为什么雨水在地表聚集并在每年春天形成一个池塘，为蚊子提供了滋生地。

def_en: relating to charity or alms (eleemosynary institution, eleemosynary fund)
def_zh: 慈善的，施舍的（慈善机构，慈善基金）
quote_en: The hospital was founded as an eleemosynary institution to provide free care to the poor, but now it charges wealthy patients exorbitant fees to subsidize the free care for everyone else.
quote_zh: 这家医院最初是作为一个为穷人提供免费护理的慈善机构建立的，但现在它向富有的患者收取高昂费用，以补贴其他人的免费护理。
ex_en: The eleemosynary work of the Victorian reformer Thomas Barnardo included building homes for destitute children, feeding the hungry, and advocating for laws to protect child laborers.
ex_zh: 维多利亚时代改革者托马斯·巴纳多的慈善工作包括为贫困儿童建造家园、给饥饿者食物以及倡导保护童工的法律。

def_en: related by blood (consanguineous marriage, consanguineous relatives)
def_zh: 血缘的，同宗的（血缘婚姻，血缘亲属）
quote_en: In some cultures, marriage between consanguineous relatives, such as first cousins, is traditional and even preferred, though geneticists warn of increased risks of recessive disorders.
quote_zh: 在某些文化中，血缘亲属之间的婚姻，如表亲结婚，是传统的，甚至是首选，尽管遗传学家警告隐性遗传疾病的风险增加。
ex_en: The royal families of Europe were so consanguineous that by the 19th century, almost every monarch was a cousin of every other, a situation that did not prevent them from going to war repeatedly.
ex_zh: 欧洲的王室家族血缘如此相近，以至于到19世纪，几乎每一位君主都是其他君主的表亲，这种情况并没有阻止他们反复开战。

def_en: clumsy or inept (maladroit handling, maladroit gesture)
def_zh: 笨拙的，不灵巧的（笨拙的处理，笨拙的手势）
quote_en: His maladroit attempt to pour the wine resulted in a red stain spreading across the white tablecloth, across his shirt, and eventually across the lap of the guest of honor.
quote_zh: 他笨拙地倒酒，结果在白色桌布上、他的衬衫上，最终在主宾的膝盖上留下了一片红色污渍。
ex_en: The diplomat’s maladroit comment about the host country’s flag — he said it looked “a bit too simple” — offended the entire delegation and set back years of careful relationship‑building.
ex_zh: 这位外交官关于东道国国旗的笨拙评论——他说它看起来“有点太简单了”——冒犯了整个代表团，使多年来的精心关系建设倒退。

def_en: impossible to avoid (ineluctable fate, ineluctable conclusion)
def_zh: 不可避免的（不可避免的命运，不可避免的结论）
quote_en: The aging athlete faced the ineluctable decline of his physical abilities, no matter how hard he trained, how many supplements he took, or how loudly he told reporters he felt “better than ever.”
quote_zh: 这位老将面临着身体能力不可避免的衰退，无论他训练多么努力，服用了多少补品，或者多么大声地告诉记者他感觉“比以往任何时候都好”。
ex_en: Given the overwhelming evidence of melting glaciers and rising sea levels, the scientists reached the ineluctable conclusion that human activity is the primary driver of climate change.
ex_zh: 鉴于冰川融化和海平面上升的压倒性证据，科学家得出了不可避免的结论：人类活动是气候变化的主要驱动因素。

def_en: an imagined society where life is terrible (totalitarian dystopia, bleak dystopia)
def_zh: 反乌托邦（极权反乌托邦， bleak反乌托邦）
quote_en: The novel depicts a dystopia in which the government controls every aspect of citizens’ lives, from their jobs to their thoughts, but the protagonist’s biggest complaint is the terrible coffee.
quote_zh: 这部小说描绘了一个反乌托邦，政府控制着公民生活的方方面面，从他们的工作到他们的思想，但主人公最大的抱怨是糟糕的咖啡。
ex_en: George Orwell’s Nineteen Eighty‑Four remains the most influential dystopia in English literature, introducing terms like “Big Brother,” “thought police,” and “doublethink” into everyday language.
ex_zh: 乔治·奥威尔的《一九八四》仍然是英语文学中最具影响力的反乌托邦，将“老大哥”、“思想警察”和“双重思想”等术语引入了日常语言。

def_en: involving a harsh, discordant mixture of sounds (cacophonous noise, cacophonous orchestra)
def_zh: 刺耳的，不和谐的（刺耳的噪音，不和谐的管弦乐队）
quote_en: The cacophonous sound of jackhammers, sirens, barking dogs, and a street musician playing the bagpipes made it impossible to concentrate on her online meditation class.
quote_zh: 手提钻、警报器、狗吠声以及一个街头音乐家吹奏风笛的刺耳声音使她无法专注于她的在线冥想课程。
ex_en: The first performance of Igor Stravinsky’s The Rite of Spring in 1913 was so cacophonous to contemporary ears that the audience rioted, throwing chairs and shouting at the orchestra.
ex_zh: 伊戈尔·斯特拉文斯基的《春之祭》在1913年的首演对当时的耳朵来说如此刺耳，以至于观众发生了骚乱，向管弦乐队扔椅子并大喊大叫。

def_en: the point in an orbit closest to the Earth (lunar perigee, at perigee)
def_zh: 近地点（月球近地点，在近地点）
quote_en: When the moon is at perigee, it appears about fourteen percent larger and thirty percent brighter than when it is at apogee, a phenomenon photographers call a “supermoon.”
quote_zh: 当月亮位于近地点时，它看起来比在远地点时大约大百分之十四、亮百分之三十，摄影师称之为“超级月亮”。
ex_en: The satellite’s orbit was deliberately designed with a low perigee so that atmospheric drag would eventually bring it down, preventing it from becoming permanent space junk.
ex_zh: 卫星的轨道被刻意设计为低近地点，以便大气阻力最终将其拖下，防止其成为永久的太空垃圾。

def_en: a figure of speech where a part stands for the whole (literary synecdoche, use synecdoche)
def_zh: 提喻（文学提喻，使用提喻）
quote_en: When people say “all hands on deck,” they are using synecdoche because “hands” stands for the whole sailors, whose feet and heads are presumably also on deck.
quote_zh: 当人们说“所有手上甲板”时，他们是在使用提喻，因为“手”代表整个水手，他们的脚和头大概也在甲板上。
ex_en: In the phrase “the crown has decided,” the word “crown” is a synecdoche for the entire monarchy or government, a usage that dates back to medieval legal documents.
ex_zh: 在短语“王冠已经决定”中，“王冠”一词是整个君主制或政府的提喻，这一用法可追溯到中世纪的 legal 文件。

def_en: the formation of a word from a sound associated with what it names (use onomatopoeia, onomatopoeic word)
def_zh: 拟声（使用拟声，拟声词）
quote_en: Words like “buzz,” “hiss,” and “boom” are examples of onomatopoeia because their pronunciation imitates the sounds they describe, a fact that delighted the kindergarten class.
quote_zh: 像“buzz”、“hiss”和“boom”这样的词是拟声的例子，因为它们的发音模仿了它们所描述的声音，这一事实让幼儿园的孩子们很高兴。
ex_en: The comic book used onomatopoeia extensively, with “BAM!” “POW!” and “WHAM!” appearing in large, colorful letters during every fight scene, much to the annoyance of the silent film purist.
ex_zh: 这本漫画书大量使用拟声，在每一场打斗场景中都用大而多彩的字母出现“BAM！”、“POW！”和“WHAM！”，这让无声电影纯粹主义者非常恼火。

def_en: a word that sounds the same as another but has a different meaning (homonym confusion, pair of homonyms)
def_zh: 同音异义词（同音异义词混淆，一对同音异义词）
quote_en: The student confused the homonyms “there,” “their,” and “they’re” so often that his English teacher started deducting points automatically, without even reading the rest of the sentence.
quote_zh: 这个学生经常混淆“there”、“their”和“they’re”这些同音异义词，以至于他的英语老师开始自动扣分，甚至不读句子的其余部分。
ex_en: The homonym “bow” can mean a weapon, a gesture of respect, the front of a ship, or a ribbon tied in a loop — a source of endless confusion for non‑native speakers of English.
ex_zh: 同音异义词“bow”可以指武器、尊重的姿势、船头或打成环的蝴蝶结——这是非英语母语者无尽困惑的源头。

def_en: a medication that reduces fever (take an antipyretic, antipyretic effect)
def_zh: 退烧药（服用退烧药，退烧效果）
quote_en: When her daughter’s fever reached 103 degrees, she gave her an antipyretic and then spent the rest of the night checking the thermometer every fifteen minutes like a nervous scientist monitoring an experiment.
quote_zh: 当女儿的发烧达到103华氏度时，她给她服用了退烧药，然后整晚剩下的时间都像紧张的科学家监测实验一样每隔十五分钟检查一次体温计。
ex_en: Before the development of modern antipyretics, doctors treated fevers with bloodletting, leeches, or wrapping patients in cold wet sheets — treatments that often killed the patient faster than the disease.
ex_zh: 在现代退烧药开发之前，医生用放血、水蛭或把病人裹在冷湿床单中来治疗发烧——这些治疗方法往往比疾病本身更快地杀死病人。

def_en: trousers worn for horse riding, tight from knee to ankle (riding jodhpurs, pair of jodhpurs)
def_zh: 马裤（骑马马裤，一条马裤）
quote_en: The wealthy equestrian wore tailored jodhpurs that cost more than most people’s monthly rent, but she still could not stop her horse from eating her hat.
quote_zh: 这位富有的马术运动员穿着定制的马裤，价格比大多数人的月租金还贵，但她仍然无法阻止她的马吃掉她的帽子。
ex_en: The city of Jodhpur in Rajasthan, India, gave its name to these riding trousers; British officers stationed there in the 19th century adopted the local style and adapted it for polo.
ex_zh: 印度拉贾斯坦邦的焦特布尔市将它的名字赋予了这种马裤；19世纪驻扎在那里的英国军官采用了当地的款式并对其进行了改编用于马球运动。

def_en: a verbal error in which initial sounds of words are swapped (unintentional spoonerism, famous spoonerism)
def_zh: 首音互换（无意的首音互换，著名的首音互换）
quote_en: When the tired news anchor said “well‑boiled icicle” instead of “well‑oiled bicycle,” the studio crew laughed so hard that the segment had to be restarted three times.
quote_zh: 当疲惫的新闻主播说“well‑boiled icicle”而不是“well‑oiled bicycle”时，演播室的工作人员笑得如此厉害，以至于该片段不得不重新开始三次。
ex_en: The Reverend William Archibald Spooner, warden of New College, Oxford, was so famous for such slips that “spoonerism” became the term; he once supposedly toasted the queen with “three cheers for our queer old dean!”
ex_zh: 牛津大学新学院院长威廉·阿奇博尔德·斯普纳牧师因这类口误而如此出名，以至于“首音互换”成了术语；据说他曾向女王祝酒说：“为我们古怪的老院长三呼万岁！”

def_en: beautiful (pulchritudinous landscape, pulchritudinous model)
def_zh: 美丽的（美丽的风景，美丽的模特）
quote_en: The photographer claimed he only took pictures of pulchritudinous subjects, but his portfolio consisted entirely of close‑ups of his own cat, which was objectively quite ordinary.
quote_zh: 摄影师声称他只拍摄美丽的主题，但他的作品集完全由他自己猫的特写组成，客观地说，这只猫相当普通。
ex_en: The word “pulchritudinous” is ironic because it sounds ugly — full of hard consonants and awkward syllables — yet it means “extremely beautiful,” a contradiction that delights linguists.
ex_zh: “pulchritudinous”这个词具有讽刺意味，因为它听起来丑陋——充满了硬辅音和笨拙的音节——却意味着“极其美丽”，这一矛盾让语言学家们感到高兴。

def_en: pleasing to the ear (euphonious melody, euphonious name)
def_zh: 悦耳的，动听的（悦耳的旋律，动听的名字）
quote_en: The band chose the euphonious name “Velvet Morning” because it sounded pleasant, but their music was so loud and dissonant that the name seemed like a cruel joke.
quote_zh: 乐队选择了悦耳的名字“Velvet Morning”，因为它听起来很愉快，但他们的音乐如此响亮和不和谐，以至于这个名字看起来像一个残酷的笑话。
ex_en: The Italian language is often described as euphonious because of its many vowel endings and melodic rhythm, which is why so many opera librettos are written in Italian.
ex_zh: 意大利语常被描述为悦耳动听，因为它有许多元音结尾和旋律节奏，这就是为什么如此多的歌剧剧本是用意大利语写的。

def_en: relating to the common people; vulgar or unsophisticated (plebeian tastes, plebeian origins)
def_zh: 平民的，粗俗的（平民的品味，平民出身）
quote_en: The aristocrat sniffed at his friend’s plebeian taste in beer — he preferred cheap lager to expensive ale — but he happily drank it when no one was looking.
quote_zh: 这位贵族对他朋友在啤酒方面的平民品味嗤之以鼻——对方更喜欢廉价啤酒而不是昂贵的麦芽酒——但当没人在看时，他高兴地喝了。
ex_en: In ancient Rome, the Conflict of the Orders was a two‑hundred‑year struggle between the patrician aristocrats and the plebeian commoners for political equality, which the plebeians eventually won.
ex_zh: 在古罗马，等级冲突是贵族和平民之间长达两百年的争取政治平等的斗争，最终平民获胜。
